category/java/bestpractices.xml/NonExhau.checker_desc: This checker detects switch statements that are not exhaustive, i.e., they lack a default case and are not exhaustive enum switches, while also excluding empty switches and pattern-matching switches where the compiler ensures exhaustiveness.

category/java/errorprone.xml/DoNotTermin.checker_desc: Detects calls to System.exit, Runtime.exit, or Runtime.halt within non-main methods or initializers.

category/java/codestyle.xml/ControlState.checker_desc: This checker detects control statements (if, else, while, for, foreach, do-while, and optionally case labels) whose body is not a block (i.e., not enclosed in braces).

category/java/bestpractices.xml/UnusedAs.checker_desc: This rule detects assignments to variables where the assigned value is never read before being overwritten or going out of scope.

category/java/multithreading.xml/DoubleC.checker_desc: Detects a non-volatile field that is lazily initialized with double-checked locking but may be observed in a partially constructed state due to improper synchronization.

category/java/errorprone.xml/OverrideBot.checker_desc: Detects when a class (including anonymous classes and records) overrides either `equals` or `hashCode` but not both, unless the class implements `Comparable`.

category/java/errorprone.xml/EmptyFinali.checker_desc: Detects empty finalize methods in non-final classes or final classes with non-final finalize methods.

category/java/errorprone.xml/FinalizeSho.checker_desc: This checker detects non-protected finalize methods with zero parameters in Java classes.

category/java/errorprone.xml/ReturnFromF.checker_desc: Detects return statements inside finally blocks that are not within a nested method or lambda declaration.

category/java/multithreading.xml/UseNoti.checker_desc: Detects calls to the `notify` method without arguments.

category/java/bestpractices.xml/UnusedFo.checker_desc: Detects unused formal parameters in private methods/constructors (or all methods/constructors if configured) unless the parameter name indicates it is intentionally unused.

category/java/codestyle.xml/UnnecessaryC.checker_desc: This rule detects unnecessary cast expressions where the cast is redundant because the operand's type already satisfies the context's requirements or the cast does not affect the semantics.

category/java/errorprone.xml/ImplicitSwi.checker_desc: Detects switch branches that implicitly fall through to the next branch without a comment indicating the fall-through is intentional.

category/java/security.xml/InsecureCrypt.checker_desc: This rule detects insecure usage of hardcoded initialization vectors (IVs) in cryptographic operations by checking for direct instantiation of IvParameterSpec with constant arguments.

category/java/errorprone.xml/StaticEJBFi.checker_desc: This checker detects static field declarations in EJB-related classes that are not declared final.

category/java/bestpractices.xml/UnusedLa.checker_desc: This checker detects labeled statements whose label is never referenced by any break or continue statement within its scope.

category/java/errorprone.xml/UseEqualsTo.checker_desc: Detects string comparisons using '==' or '!=' operators instead of the equals method.

category/java/errorprone.xml/NonSerializ.checker_desc: Detects non-serializable fields in Serializable classes that are not handled by custom serialization methods or serialPersistentFields.

category/java/errorprone.xml/AvoidCallin.checker_desc: Detects calls to Object.finalize() that are not inside a finalize method.

category/java/bestpractices.xml/UnusedPr.checker_desc: Detects private fields that are declared but never used within the same compilation unit.

category/java/multithreading.xml/DontCal.checker_desc: Detects direct calls to the run() method of java.lang.Thread instances, which bypasses proper thread scheduling and executes the method in the current thread instead of starting a new thread.

category/java/errorprone.xml/AvoidCatchi.checker_desc: This checker detects catch clauses that catch generic exception types like Exception, RuntimeException, Throwable, or Error.

category/java/errorprone.xml/CompareObje.checker_desc: This checker detects equality comparisons (== or !=) between two non-null object expressions that are not of types that compare by reference, unless the comparison is inside an equals method or involves a field/variable with an all-caps name.
